key_insight: A viscoplastic plus gradient-damage constitutive framework can capture both nonlinear deformation and fracture in additively manufactured polymer TPMS lattices.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable in silico design of lightweight lattice structures with improved prediction of failure under tension, compression, and cyclic/non-monotonic loading.
method: Implemented a high-fidelity rate-dependent viscoplastic model in Abaqus/Explicit via UMAT.
Added a damage initiation criterion based on stored elastic energy and equivalent plastic strain.
Coupled damage evolution using a gradient-damage framework and validated it against experiments.
evidence_signal: The model was validated qualitatively and quantitatively against experiments on a unit cell under non-monotonic loading and lattices under tension.
watch_item: The framework is validated on specific TPMS geometries and loading cases, so broader generalization and parameter transferability may require further testing.
paper_title: Modeling damage and fracture in additively manufactured polymeric triply periodic minimal surface lattices
